name: orc-war-camp
summary: An orc war-band's forward camp — crude but organized. Totems of skulls and painted hide, a central bonfire, rows of sleeping mats under hide-tarps, butchery pit, weapon stacks. Discipline under the filth.

primary_palette.note: huge central bonfire, skull totems at cardinal points, warriors' kit around
totem gateway leading into the camp — bonfire beyond
messy butchery corner of the camp — raw meat, hides drying
rough throne of logs and furs — the warchief's seat

story_prompts: full warband in camp — loud, drinking, the night before an attack
mid-raid — only skeleton crew, slaves and wounded — warband gone, back soon
the chief is dead — the totems have been knocked over, infighting underway
a prisoner cage holds someone the party needs alive
not orcs — this is a half-orc warband that disciplined itself into a real army

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor night — ambient low, bonfire dominates, torches on totems

anti_patterns: no built walls — the camp is a cleared outdoor space, defined by totems and tarps
do not make it random — orcs DO organize, just crudely (sleeping rows, weapon stacks, fire hierarchy)
totems should be large and visible, not scatter-scale — they are landmarks
bonfire sits central, chieftain's seat faces it — social geometry matters
